chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Cr. Bail Application No.S-735 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 22.08.2022. Applicants are present on ad-interim pre-arrest bail. Mr. Muzammil Bughio, Advocate holds brief for applicants’ Counsel. Mr. Hussain Bux Solangi, Advocate a/w complainant. Ms. Sana Memon, Assistant P.G. O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J- Applicants allegedly armed with firearm weapons assaulted wife of complainant in his presence causing her severe blows on different parts of her body and tearing her clothes on 11.06.2022 near village Ashraf Lund District Dadu on account of previous enmity. The injuries sustained by her are minor in nature as 337-L(ii) PPC, bailable and punishable upto two years. The charge under Section 354-A PPC is yet to be determined as prima facie there is insufficient material in support of this charge. Besides, there is delay of three days in registration of FIR which does not seem to be plausibly explained. Applicants’ Counsel is called absent and Mr. Muzammil Bughio, Advocate holds brief, therefore, I have heard the applicants in person. 2. Learned Counsel for the complainant has opposed bail to the applicants. However, learned Assistant Prosecutor General has stated that injuries sustained by victim are bailable and more so it is strange that her husband was present at the time of incident but he did not try to save her and/or has received any bruise and as such the case requires further inquiry. Accordingly, in view of what has been discussed above, this application is allowed and ad-interim pre-arrest bail already granted to the applicants is hereby confirmed on same terms and conditions. 3.

However, learned Assistant Prosecutor General has stated that injuries sustained by victim are bailable and more so it is strange that her husband was present at the time of incident but he did not try to save her and/or has received any bruise and as such the case requires further inquiry. Accordingly, in view of what has been discussed above, this application is allowed and ad-interim pre-arrest bail already granted to the applicants is hereby confirmed on same terms and conditions. 3. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and shall not influence the trial Court while deciding the case on merits. JUDGE Shahid
